2006-09-01.title: Baja Battens Down as Hurricane John Advances
2006-09-01.abstract: Winds from Hurricane John reached 115 miles an hour early today as it advanced toward this tourist resort.

2006-09-01.title: Israel’s Defense Chief Supports War Inquiry
2006-09-01.abstract: Defense Minister Amir Peretz called today for a full independent inquiry into the war in Lebanon, putting him at odds with Prime Minister Ehud Olmert.

2006-09-01.title: Pentagon Releases Grim Report on Iraq
2006-09-01.abstract: Iraqi casualties soared by more than 50 percent during the roughly three-month period ending in early August, the report said.

2006-09-01.title: Britain’s Political Season Gets Under Way
2006-09-01.abstract: The prime minister said he would not be quitting any time soon, while his adversaries within his own party said he should.

2006-09-01.title: Russia Gets Tougher on Illegal Downloading
2006-09-01.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Friday.

2006-09-02.title: Iraqi Casualties Are Up Sharply, Study Finds
2006-09-02.abstract: The Pentagon’s latest comprehensive assessment of security in Iraq pointed to sectarian violence and a “potent” insurgency.

2006-09-02.title: Plane Landing in Northeastern Iran Catches Fire, Killing 29
2006-09-02.abstract: The Russian-made aircraft burst into flames when one of its tires blew out and the plane slipped off the runway.

2006-09-02.title: Hurricane Makes Landfall at Tip of Baja California
2006-09-02.abstract: Hurricane John, one of the strongest storms to threaten the Baja California peninsula in years, crashed ashore on the eastern tip of the peninsula.

2006-09-02.title: Pakistan: Tribal Leader Quickly Buried
2006-09-02.abstract: Despite a strong protest by his relatives, the government buried what they said were the remains of Nawab Akbar Khan Bugti, the 79-year-old tribal leader killed a week ago when the Pakistan military brought down his hide-out cave in southern Baluchistan Province, in his hometown of Dera Bugti. None of Mr. Bugti’s relatives attended the state-managed burial. His sons and relatives had refused to go to Dera Bugti and instead asked the government to hand over the body in Quetta, the provincial capital. Government officials refused, saying the body was fast decomposing. On Thursday, military officials said they unearthed the body of Mr. Bugti, whose death had set off days of protests and rioting, from under a heavy boulder in the cave. Officials showed journalists his reading glasses and wristwatch; they had no scratches.

2006-09-02.title: Japan’s Likely Next Premier in Hawkish Stand
2006-09-02.abstract: Shinzo Abe, declaring his candidacy, said that Japan should revise the pacifist Constitution imposed on it by the United States.

2006-09-03.title: Iraq Reports Capture of Senior Al Qaeda Figure
2006-09-03.abstract: Hamid Juma Faris Jouri al-Saeedi supervised the bombing of a revered Shiite shrine in February that set off a wave of brutal sectarian violence.

2006-09-03.title: 14 South Asian Shiite Pilgrims Shot to Death in Iraqi Desert
2006-09-03.abstract: The violence contributed to a death toll of at least 25 civilians around Iraq in a period of 24 hours, officials said.

2006-09-03.title: Storm Weakens, but Sends Torrents of Water Across Baja Peninsula
2006-09-03.abstract: A weakened but still dangerous Hurricane John lashed the southern part of the Baja California peninsula with fierce winds and rain.

2006-09-03.title: Many Panamanians Say No to Canal Upgrade
2006-09-03.abstract: A referendum to modernize the congested Panama Canal may fail because of concerns about the cost of the project and fears of graft.

2006-09-03.title: France Deports More Illegal Immigrants, but Shies From Children
2006-09-03.abstract: After suburban riots last fall, the interior minister vowed to step up the pace of expulsions that have included mainly Africans, Asians and East Europeans.

2006-09-04.title: Investigator Recommends Courts-Martial for 4 Soldiers
2006-09-04.abstract: The charges could lead to the death penalty for the four U.S. infantrymen accused of raping a 14-year-old girl and killing her and her family in Iraq last March.

2006-09-04.title: Annan Says Iran’s President Holds to Nuclear Program
2006-09-04.abstract: Iran is willing to negotiate but will not suspend uranium enrichment beforehand, the U.N. secretary general said.

2006-09-04.title: New Assault Takes Big Toll on Taliban, NATO Says
2006-09-04.abstract: Reports estimated that as many as 200 Taliban fighters were killed in a new offensive in southern Afghanistan.

2006-09-04.title: Police Search Islamic School in South Britain
2006-09-04.abstract: British police were continuing the hunt for jihadist recruiters and trainers that led to 14 arrests in London last week.

2006-09-04.title: Troops Cut Death, but Not Fear, in Baghdad Zone
2006-09-04.abstract: Dora, the first of several violent neighborhoods covered by a new Baghdad security plan, represents the embryo of progress.

2006-09-05.title: Annan Working to Lift Israeli Blockade of Lebanon
2006-09-05.abstract: A plan could lead to a lifting of the blockade within 48 hours, the secretary general said.

2006-09-05.title: Calderón Named Mexico’s President-Elect
2006-09-05.abstract: An electoral tribunal today declared the conservative candidate Felipe Calderón the next president, rejecting arguments from a leftist candidate that the election should be annulled.

2006-09-05.title: African Union Threatens to Leave Darfur
2006-09-05.abstract: The African Union said it would quit the region by the end of the month if Sudan did not agree to allow a U.N. force to take over.

2006-09-05.title: Africa’s Business Reform Praised by World Bank
2006-09-05.abstract: African nations have made surprising strides in carrying out changes that make it easier to start and run a business, a new report says.

2006-09-05.title: An Afghan Symbol for Change, Then Failure
2006-09-05.abstract: Lashkar Gah is today the epicenter of a Taliban resurgence and an explosion in drug cultivation.

2006-09-06.title: Cuba: Message From Castro
2006-09-06.abstract: Photographs of a gaunt but alert Fidel Castro appeared in Cuba’s official newspaper with a signed note from the recuperating leader saying “the most critical moment is behind me.” Mr. Castro, 80, who turned over power to his brother on July 31, said that he was 41 pounds lighter after intestinal surgery but was on the mend and would participate in next week’s Non-Aligned Movement summit meeting.

2006-09-06.title: In Africa, a More Business-Friendly Approach
2006-09-06.abstract: African nations have made surprising strides in carrying out changes that make it easier to start and run a business, a new report says.

2006-09-06.title: Nuclear Déjà Vu: Now It’s Iran That Does or Doesn’t Intend to Make Nuclear Weapons
2006-09-06.abstract: The Bush administration and the U.N. are in a parallel situation to where they were four years ago this month, in the period leading up to the war in Iraq.

2006-09-06.title: Israel to Lift Blockade of Lebanon
2006-09-06.abstract: Israel said it was assured that Lebanese and international forces would stop arms from being smuggled to Hezbollah.

2006-09-06.title: U.S. Air Force Officer Missing in Kyrgyzstan
2006-09-06.abstract: A United States Air Force officer stationed at an air base in Kyrgyzstan was reported missing today after disappearing during a shopping trip on Tuesday with fellow service members in the capital, Kyrgyz and American officials said.

2006-09-07.title: Bosnia: War-Crimes Witness Found Dead
2006-09-07.abstract: Prosecutors at the war crimes tribunal in The Hague are seeking information about the apparent suicide of a crucial witness, Stevan Todorovic, 49, a former wartime police chief in Bosnia, who was found over the weekend at his home with a gunshot wound to his temple and a pistol in his hand. This is the second death of a top witness this year. Milan Babic, a former prime minister, hanged himself in his cell in The Hague in March. Both men were to testify at the trial of two former heads of the Serbian security service.

2006-09-07.title: 2 Muslims Are Charged in Plot for a Bombing in Denmark
2006-09-07.abstract: Of nine suspects arrested, only two were formally charged on Wednesday, and the police said they are scrambling to hold the case together.

2006-09-07.title: Blair Says He Plans to Resign in Next Year
2006-09-07.abstract: The British prime minister’s announcement comes a day after an extraordinary procession of eight junior aides resigned.

2006-09-07.title: U.S. Air Force Major Missing in Kyrgyzstan
2006-09-07.abstract: The officer stationed in Kyrgyzstan was reported missing on Wednesday after disappearing during a shopping trip.

2006-09-07.title: Musharraf Vows to Aid Afghanistan in Fighting Taliban
2006-09-07.abstract: While visiting Afghanistan, Pakistan’s president promised to help combat the Taliban insurgency, which operates from his country.

2006-09-08.title: Mental Activity Seen in a Brain Gravely Injured
2006-09-08.abstract: The finding could have far-reaching consequences for how unconscious patients are cared for and diagnosed.

2006-09-08.title: French Opposition Backs Lebanon Role; Military Has Doubts
2006-09-08.abstract: France’s opposition parties embraced the decision to deploy troops to Lebanon, even as military commanders remained troubled.

2006-09-08.title: Lebanese Ports Still Blocked
2006-09-08.abstract: The air travel embargo ended Thursday, but restrictions will remain at sea until international naval forces are ready.

2006-09-08.title: Israel: Woman to Lead Supreme Court
2006-09-08.abstract: An Israeli judicial panel selected Dorit Beinish as the first woman to be president of Israel’s Supreme Court, replacing Aharon Barak, who is retiring after nearly 30 years on the country’s highest court. Ms. Beinish, 64, is a longtime civil servant who has held a range of government legal posts and was appointed to the Supreme Court in 1995. Mr. Barak, 70, had been president since 1995.

2006-09-08.title: Blast Kills 16 in Afghan Capital, Including 2 U.S. Soldiers
2006-09-08.abstract: A suicide bomber smashed a car into an American military vehicle just yards from the U.S. embassy in downtown Kabul.

2006-09-09.title: Poland: Asian Politician May Have Been Framed
2006-09-09.abstract: An opposition member of the Kyrgyz Parliament, Omurbek Tejebayev, who was arrested at the Warsaw airport earlier this week on drug charges, was released after security videotape suggested that the heroin that police found in a doll in his luggage may have been planted there. Mr. Tekebayev has publicly feuded with and insulted the Kyrgyz president.

2006-09-09.title: Georgia: Fighting Flares in Breakaway Region
2006-09-09.abstract: At least four people were killed as fresh fighting broke out between Georgia and the separatist region of South Ossetia. South Ossetia, which has tacit backing from Russia, said Georgian interior troops attacked a village checkpoint, killing three Ossetian police officers; the Georgian government said its troops were chasing smugglers and that one Georgian officer was killed. Tensions in the region rose even higher than usual after gunfire struck a helicopter carrying the Georgian defense minister on Sept. 2.

2006-09-09.title: Britain: Top of the Scofflaw List
2006-09-09.abstract: The mayor of London, Ken Livingstone, said the United States Embassy owed $1.6 million in unpaid traffic-congestion charges and fines, making it the worst offender in the city. Embassy employees have not paid the charges — £8 ($15) a day for any car entering central London — since July 2005, arguing that the charge is a tax; diplomats are exempt from taxes. But London officials say the charge is a toll, not a tax, and say that British diplomats pay tolls in America.

2006-09-09.title: Suicide Bomber Kills 16 in Kabul Near Embassy
2006-09-09.abstract: The Kabul bombing came amid a sharp escalation of violence in Afghanistan.

2006-09-09.title: Slovenian President Finds Peace and Wants to Share It
2006-09-09.abstract: After surviving cancer, Janez Drnovsek said he was suddenly emboldened to speak his mind on all sorts of topics.

2006-09-10.title: Israeli Says Fatah Leader Could Help Free Soldier
2006-09-10.abstract: In an apparent policy shift, Israel’s prime minister said he was prepared to meet the Palestinian Authority president even without the release of a captured Israeli soldier.

2006-09-10.title: Afghan Governor Killed in Suicide Attack
2006-09-10.abstract: Muhammad Hakim Taniwal of Paktia Province was the first governor to be killed since the Taliban began a campaign of suicide bombings last year.

2006-09-10.title: Darfur Trembles as Peacekeepers’ Exit Looms
2006-09-10.abstract: Fear is mounting in refugee camps as it grows more likely that African Union peacekeepers will leave or be ejected.

2006-09-10.title: Montenegro Votes, With Post-Serbia Path Unclear
2006-09-10.abstract: The real mystery is how Montenegro’s prime minister will lead the country into a future free of the baggage of the former Yugoslavia.

2006-09-10.title: Air Force Officer Leaves Kyrgyzstan
2006-09-10.abstract: A U.S. Air Force officer missing in Kyrgyzstan was flown from the country, while the investigation into her disappearance continued.

2006-09-11.title: Montenegro Premier Appears to Win Vote
2006-09-11.abstract: Prime Minister Milo Djukanovic appears to have secured four more years in office, according to unofficial results.

2006-09-11.title: Immediate Treatment Needed for Bird Flu Cases, Study Says
2006-09-11.abstract: A study found that the avian flu virus reproduces so quickly that if not suppressed within 48 hours, it pushes victims toward death.

2006-09-11.title: King Taufa’ahau Tupou IV, Ruler of Tonga, Dies at 88
2006-09-11.abstract: King Taufa’ahau Tupou IV of Tonga was the last reigning monarch in the South Pacific.

2006-09-11.title: China Puts Stricter Limits on Distribution of Foreign News
2006-09-11.abstract: Under the new rules, the state-run news agency would become the  de facto gatekeeper for foreign news entering China.

2006-09-11.title: Blair and Abbas Discuss Idea of a Unity Government
2006-09-11.abstract: The British prime minister said a Palestinian unity government could receive widespread support if it met conditions set by the U.N. and Western countries.

2006-09-12.title: An Unusual 9/11 Tribute
2006-09-12.abstract: Four years ago, Masai tribesmen in Enoosaen, Kenya, offered a special gift to the United States to help ease the pain of the Sept. 11 attacks. (Producer: Kassie Bracken)

2006-09-12.title: Bomber Attacks Afghan Governor’s Funeral
2006-09-12.abstract: At the funeral of a governor who was assassinated the day before, a suicide bomber killed at least seven people.

2006-09-12.title: Israel: Settler Guilty in Rampage
2006-09-12.abstract: A Jewish settler who is believed to have hoped to halt Israel’s withdrawal from the Gaza Strip last year was convicted in Jerusalem for the August 2005 shooting deaths of four Palestinians in the West Bank. The man, Asher Weissgan, 41, killed the Palestinians the day Israel began evacuating settlers from Gaza.

2006-09-12.title: Japan: Better Spying From Above
2006-09-12.abstract: Japan launched its third intelligence-gathering satellite, increasing its ability to spy on its neighbors. Even with a fourth satellite planned in a few months, it is expected to remain largely dependent on the United States for information.

2006-09-12.title: 10 Dead in Crash of Military Helicopter
2006-09-12.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Tuesday.

2006-09-13.title: Kyrgyzstan: President’s Brother Ousted
2006-09-13.abstract: The head of the intelligence service and a deputy, a brother of President Kurmanbek Bakiyev, left office after the president’s brother was accused of planting heroin in luggage of an opposition leader before a flight to Poland. The opposition leader, Omurbek Tekebayev, was held in Warsaw for several days after the heroin was found. An airport official said Mr. Bakiyev’s brother, Zhanysh Bakiyev, had arranged for the luggage to be tampered with. Bussurmankul Tabaldiyev, chairman of the intelligence service, resigned.

2006-09-13.title: 30 British Unionists Snub Blair
2006-09-13.abstract: About 30 hard-liners walked out of the annual conference of British labor unions as Prime Minister Tony Blair rose to speak. Bob Crow, leader of the left-wing Rail, Maritime and Transport Union, said the walkout, which was widely expected, had been to protest the deployment of British troops in Iraq, Afghanistan and elsewhere and to object to the union’s expulsion from the ruling Labor Party. But at the conference Mr. Blair received important support from Gordon Brown, his rival and heir apparent, who said he supported the prime minister’s efforts to reform the economy and public services.

2006-09-13.title: Survivor of Attack on U.S. Embassy Dies in Syria
2006-09-13.abstract: The sole survivor of the four-man team that attacked the American embassy here Tuesday died of his wounds without being questioned, Syria’s official news agency reported.

2006-09-13.title: Dance of Diplomacy Is Grist for the Gossip Mill
2006-09-13.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice’s appearances with her Canadian counterpart have revived rumors of romance on the diplomatic circuit.

2006-09-13.title: More Financing Is Urged to Fight TB in Africa
2006-09-13.abstract: The World Bank is spending far less on proven methods to combat tuberculosis in Africa than in India, China and Russia, according to a report by an advocacy group.

2006-09-14.title: Pierre Vidal-Naquet, 76, Dies; French Opponent of Torture
2006-09-14.abstract: Mr. Vidal-Naquet, an eminent historian of the ancient world, became widely known for exposing wartime atrocities of the modern one.

2006-09-14.title: On Another Grim Day, Bodies Lie Everywhere in Baghdad
2006-09-14.abstract: As Iraqi police gathered up at least 60 bodies, several car bombs rocked the capital, killing and wounding dozens more.

2006-09-14.title: Scholar Revises Terrorism Research Plan
2006-09-14.abstract: An Australian academic who was given a $625,000 grant by a federal agency for a study on terrorism and suicide bombers has modified his proposal after the attorney general warned him that his research might run afoul of new antiterrorism laws. The academic, Riaz Hassan, a retired professor of sociology at The Flinders University of South Australia, planned to interview leaders of organizations like Hezbollah, Hamas and Jemaah Islamiyah in Indonesia. The attorney general, Philip Ruddock, said the interviews might violate laws that make it a crime to associate with terrorists.

2006-09-14.title: In the Netherlands, Life Runs on 2 Wheels (Sometimes 3)
2006-09-14.abstract: The Dutch are turning to bicycles in ever greater numbers. Sales are booming, and there is a proliferation of designs for all sorts of purposes.

2006-09-14.title: Contraband Inquiry Targets 19 Russian Security Officers
2006-09-14.abstract: A summary of the top stories in Russian newspapers on Thursday.

2006-09-15.title: Hope in Uganda
2006-09-15.abstract: Two weeks after a cease-fire agreement, villagers in Gulu, Uganda, remain scarred but hopeful for peace. (Producers: Courtenay Morris and Kassie Bracken)

2006-09-15.title: Nuclear Agency for U.N. Faults Report on Iran by U.S. House
2006-09-15.abstract: The International Atomic Energy Agency said the House Intelligence Committee report “contains erroneous, misleading and unsubstantiated information’’ about Iran’s nuclear program.

2006-09-15.title: Germany: Syrian Suspect in Train Plot Is Released
2006-09-15.abstract: A Syrian man arrested in connection with a foiled plot to blow up commuter trains has been released, the German general prosecutor said in a statement. Fadi al-Saleh, 23, had been suspected of helping two Lebanese men to place two suitcase bombs on trains in the cities of Dortmund and Koblenz on July 31. The federal court in Karlsruhe cited lack of evidence against Mr. Saleh as it ordered his release.

2006-09-15.title: South Korean Still Leading in U.N. Poll
2006-09-15.abstract: Ban Ki-moon, South Korea’s foreign minister, won for the second time in a Security Council straw poll of announced candidates to succeed Kofi Annan as secretary general. As in the first secret balloting of the 15 Council members on July 24, Mr. Ban was first, Shashi Tharoor of India, the under secretary general for communications, was second, and Surakiart Sathirathai, Thailand’s deputy prime minister, was third. Fourth was Prince Zeid al-Hussein, Jordan’s ambassador to the United Nations, who announced his candidacy last week, and fifth was Jayantha Dhanapala of Sri Lanka, former under secretary general for disarmament. More candidates are expected to enter the contest, which will be decided in October when the Council is to recommend one name to the General Assembly for ratification.

2006-09-15.title: Toll of Darfur Underreported, Study Declares
2006-09-15.abstract: The death toll in Sudan’s Darfur conflict has reached into the hundreds of thousands, according to an article in the journal Science.

2006-09-16.title: Zimbabwe: Union Leader Beaten by Police
2006-09-16.abstract: A top leader of Zimbabwe’s Congress of Trade Unions was in a hospital in Harare, the capital, after being beaten by police officers who violently broke up a union protest against the government’s economic and civil liberties policies, his lawyer said. The union official, Wellington Chibebe, was one of 15 protesters taken to Parirenyatwa Hospital under police guard; the other 14 were treated and returned to a police jail, the lawyer, Alec Muchadehma, told Agence France-Presse.

2006-09-16.title: Hardly a Revolution, but Swedes Could Evict Leaders
2006-09-16.abstract: Sweden holds its most closely contested national election in years on Sunday, with unemployment the key issue.

2006-09-16.title: W.H.O. Supports Wider Use of DDT vs. Malaria
2006-09-16.abstract: The World Health Organization said the insecticide poses no risk when sprayed in small amounts on the inner walls of homes.

2006-09-16.title: Secrets and Lies Shroud Origins of Giant Swastika
2006-09-16.abstract: The provenance of a man-made forest in the shape of a swastika near the edge of the Himalayas presents a complicated mystery.

2006-09-16.title: Iraqis Plan to Ring Baghdad With Trenches
2006-09-16.abstract: The violent city will be  sealed off with a series of trenches and traffic checkpoints to control movement in and out.

2006-09-17.title: Pope Apologizes for Uproar Over His Remarks
2006-09-17.abstract: Church experts said it appeared to be first time a pope has made such a direct apology.

2006-09-17.title: As Deaths Rise, Sunnis Criticize Baghdad Plan
2006-09-17.abstract: The rising death toll has prompted Sunni leaders to question the security operation intended to take Baghdad back from insurgents.

2006-09-17.title: Drug Can Prevent Diabetes in Many at High Risk, Study Suggests
2006-09-17.abstract: A new study found that the drug rosiglitazone reduced the chance of developing diabetes for people in high-risk categories by two-thirds.

2006-09-17.title: Israel Sets Up Panel to Examine Lebanon War
2006-09-17.abstract: Many Israelis, including a large number of disgruntled reserve soldiers, have been demanding an inquiry.

2006-09-17.title: Iraqi Tribes to Join Forces to Fight Insurgents
2006-09-17.abstract: An influential tribal leader announced the agreement to fight Al Qaeda insurgents today, while in Kirkuk, suicide bombers killed over two dozen people.

2006-09-18.title: Many Muslims Say Pope’s Apology Is Inadequate
2006-09-18.abstract: The pope had only said he was “sorry” for the “reaction” to a speech discussing Islam last week, noted some Muslims.

2006-09-18.title: Sweden’s Governing Party Voted Out After 12 Years
2006-09-18.abstract: Voters rejected Sweden’s longtime prime minister for a conservative who has pledged to revise the welfare state.

2006-09-18.title: Resumption of Sri Lanka War Tests Civilians’ Endurance
2006-09-18.abstract: New battles have grown into full-scale military exchanges and are the deadliest outbreak since a 2002 truce.

2006-09-18.title: Islamists’ Rise Imperils Mideast’s Order
2006-09-18.abstract: From Egypt to Saudi Arabia, the order that emerged after World War II has seen its influence challenged in the wave of instability washing across the Middle East.

2006-09-18.title: Israeli Visa Policy Traps Thousands of Palestinians in a Legal Quandary
2006-09-18.abstract: An Israeli immigration policy that banned renewable tourist visas has left thousands with no legal way to remain in Palestinian areas.

2006-09-19.title: Spain and Britain Reach Gibraltar Accords
2006-09-19.abstract: The agreements do not address the central question: Spain’s objections to British control of the territory.

2006-09-19.title: Iran’s Freeze on Enrichment Could Wait, France Suggests
2006-09-19.abstract: France’s president suggested that Iran would not have to freeze major nuclear activities until formal negotiations began.

2006-09-19.title: British Soldier Pleads Guilty to War Crime
2006-09-19.abstract: The plea came at the opening of a court martial of seven soldiers revolving around events six months after the invasion of Iraq.

2006-09-19.title: Soccer Skirmish Turns Spotlight on Brazil’s Racial Divide
2006-09-19.abstract: After a police officer called a black referee a “monkey,” a lesson in the complexities of Brazil’s racial politics followed.

2006-09-19.title: Chief Judge in Hussein’s Genocide Trial Removed
2006-09-19.abstract: Iraq’s prime minister decided to remove the judge after he said last week that Saddam Hussein was “not a dictator.”

2006-09-20.title: Iranian Leader Accepts Efforts by Pope to Recast His Remarks
2006-09-20.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad of Iran suggested today that Pope Benedict XVI had satisfactorily “modified” his remarks on Islam.

2006-09-20.title: Yemen Leader Is Now Paying for Providing Open Election
2006-09-20.abstract: In Yemen’s landmark presidential election, President Ali Abdullah Saleh is fending off the biggest electoral challenge of his 28-year rule.

2006-09-20.title: Chávez Calls Bush ‘the Devil’ in U.N. Speech
2006-09-20.abstract: President Hugo Chávez of Venezuela bitterly assailed President Bush in a speech at the United Nations.

2006-09-20.title: With Premier at U.N., Thai Military Stages Coup
2006-09-20.abstract: Leaders of Thailand’s armed forces seized control of Bangkok, suspended the constitution and declared martial law on Tuesday.

2006-09-20.title: $50 Million Offer Aims at Curbing Efforts to Make Nuclear Fuel
2006-09-20.abstract: Warren E. Buffett wants to set up an international nuclear fuel bank from which countries could buy reactor fuel instead of making it on their own.

2006-09-21.title: South Africa Drops Charge of Corruption for Official
2006-09-21.abstract: The decision put Jacob Zuma one step closer to seizing control of the African National Congress party and, eventually, the nation’s presidency.

2006-09-21.title: In Reversal, Germany Will Join Lebanon Force
2006-09-21.abstract: Germany approved sending 2,400 troops to patrol the waters off Lebanon on Wednesday, the country’s first deployment to the Middle East since World War II.

2006-09-21.title: Indonesia Executes Three Christians
2006-09-21.abstract: Three Christians convicted in connection with anti-Muslim communal violence in 2000 were executed in spite of protests from the European Union.

2006-09-21.title: Iran’s Leader Challenges U.N. on Hezbollah
2006-09-21.abstract: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad refused to say whether he would comply with a demand to withhold arms from Hezbollah.

2006-09-21.title: On First Day, New Judge Throws Hussein Out of Court, to Lawyers’ Dismay
2006-09-21.abstract: The new judge, Muhammad al-Uraibi, was clearly determined to show he would not tolerate outbursts by Saddam Hussein.

2006-09-22.title: Musharraf Defends Deal With Tribal Leaders
2006-09-22.abstract: Pakistan’s president tried to convince President Bush that he was not backing away from fighting the Taliban.

2006-09-22.title: Iran Leader, at U.N., Skirts Issue of Hezbollah’s Disarmament
2006-09-22.abstract: Iran’s president, Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, refused to say if he would meet a Security Council demand to disarm and disband Hezbollah.

2006-09-22.title: Hezbollah Chief Leads Huge Rally
2006-09-22.abstract: Sheikh Hassan Nasrallah told a crowd in Beirut that the militant group had 20,000 rockets and would never disarm.

2006-09-22.title: 5 Palestinians Are Killed by Israeli Strikes in Gaza
2006-09-22.abstract: The Israeli military said it attacked Palestinians spotted handling a rocket launcher after it had been used to fire on Israel.

2006-09-22.title: Istanbul Court Clears Author of Insulting Turkish Identity
2006-09-22.abstract: In a case monitored by the European Union, the court dropped charges against Elif Shafak because of a lack of evidence.

2006-09-23.title: Peru: New Conviction for Ex-Spy Chief
2006-09-23.abstract: A tribunal sentenced Vladimiro Montesinos, Peru’s former intelligence chief, to 20 years in prison for brokering a deal to send 10,000 assault rifles to Colombian guerrillas. Judges ruled on evidence that put Mr. Montesinos, 61, in the middle of a complex web of negotiations to bring rifles from Jordan to the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia. Mr. Montesinos, the spymaster under former President Alberto K. Fujimori, is already serving a separate 15-year term on corruption charges at a high-security naval prison in the port of Callao.

2006-09-23.title: Hungary’s Leader Vows to Survive and Revive Economy
2006-09-23.abstract: Prime Minister Ferenc Gyurcsany said it was his intention to solve the economic problems his administration covered up.

2006-09-23.title: Hamas Rebuffs Palestinian President Over Israel
2006-09-23.abstract: Hamas leaders said Friday that the group would not be part of a Palestinian government that recognizes Israel.

2006-09-23.title: European Faults U.S. Official for Remarks on Geneva Rules
2006-09-23.abstract: The European counterterrorism chief criticized the suggestion that the Geneva Conventions should not be considered sacrosanct.

2006-09-23.title: 25 Die in Crash of German High-Speed Train
2006-09-23.abstract: The crash was a setback to magnetic levitation technology, which aims to narrow the speed gap between trains and planes.

2006-09-24.title: Top Democrat in Hong Kong Will Not Run
2006-09-24.abstract: Anson Chan, a popular democracy advocate, said she would not run for chief executive, clearing the way for the re-election of Beijing’s choice for the position.

2006-09-24.title: Spy Agencies Say Iraq War Worsens Terrorism Threat
2006-09-24.abstract: A stark assessment has found that the American invasion and occupation of Iraq has helped spawn a new generation of Islamic radicalism and that the overall terrorist threat has grown since the Sept. 11 attacks.

2006-09-24.title: Lebanon’s Three-Sided Postwar Game: Who Gets Shabaa Farms?
2006-09-24.abstract: The Lebanese insist a disputed 10-mile-square area of land is theirs, and Hezbollah says it will not lay down its weapons until Israel gives it back.

2006-09-24.title: Putin Rejects Europe’s Fears on Company
2006-09-24.abstract: Europeans fear that Russia will use its increasing stake in a large European aerospace corporation to change the organization.

2006-09-24.title: In Raid, Greek Police Recover 700-Year-Old Stolen Icon
2006-09-24.abstract: A Romanian confessed to having hidden the icon, depicting the Virgin Mary holding the baby Jesus, after staging a robbery at the Elona monastery.

2006-09-25.title: Pope Meets Muslims in Bid to Defuse Anger
2006-09-25.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI told ambassadors from 22 Muslim countries that “intercultural dialogue is a necessity.”

2006-09-25.title: Zimbabwe’s Leaders Want to Delay Presidential Vote Until 2010
2006-09-25.abstract: The move would potentially give the nation’s longtime leader, Robert G. Mugabe, an extra two years in office.

2006-09-25.title: Study of Iraq War and Terror Stirs Strong Political Response
2006-09-25.abstract: Democrats called for a new war strategy after a report found the conflict in Iraq has invigorated Islamic radicalism.

2006-09-25.title: As Iraqi Lights Flicker, ‘Generator Man’ Feels Heat
2006-09-25.abstract: When the power goes out in Baghdad, Iraqis blame the generator man, probably the most vilified figure after Saddam Hussein.

2006-09-25.title: British Troops in Iraq Kill Senior Qaeda Figure
2006-09-25.abstract: Omar al-Faruq, who engineered a brazen escape from a high security American prison in Afghanistan last year, was killed in a raid today.

2006-09-26.title: Farming Bluefin Tuna, Through Thick Stocks and Thin
2006-09-26.abstract: A life’s work led to the holy grail of fish breeding: bluefin tuna, a delicacy at Japanese restaurants, born and raised in captivity.

2006-09-26.title: Israeli Premier and Saudi Said to Hold Secret Meeting
2006-09-26.abstract: If a meeting did take place, it would indicate a significant development for two countries that have never had diplomatic ties.

2006-09-26.title: Vatican Excommunicates Zambian Archbishop
2006-09-26.abstract: Archbishop Emmanuel Milingo, 76, had installed four married men as bishops in a breakaway Catholic sect.

2006-09-26.title: In Book, Musharraf Expands on North Korean Nuclear Link
2006-09-26.abstract: The assertion by President Pervez Musharraf of Pakistan deepened the mystery about North Korea’s nuclear program.

2006-09-26.title: Blair Rival Makes Pitch to Lead Labor Party
2006-09-26.abstract: Gordon Brown on Monday opened what could be the final chapter of his campaign to replace Tony Blair as Britain’s prime minister.

2006-09-27.title: Backing Policy, President Issues Terror Estimate
2006-09-27.abstract: Excerpts released Tuesday show that intelligence agencies found that the “Iraq conflict has become the ‘cause célèbre’ for jihadists.”

2006-09-27.title: Study Doesn’t Share Bush’s Optimism on Terror Fight
2006-09-27.abstract: A newly declassified report describes a jihadist movement that, for now, is simply outpacing President Bush’s counterattacks.

2006-09-27.title: Judge Postpones Hussein Trial as Lawyers Continue Boycott
2006-09-27.abstract: After throwing Saddam Hussein out of court again on Tuesday, the judge  postponed the deposed dictator’s genocide trial until Oct. 9.

2006-09-27.title: A Career Seasoned With Cigar Smoke and Revolution
2006-09-27.abstract: José Orlando Padrón, who believes that cigar smoke brings him luck, has puffed his way through some challenging situations over the years.

2006-09-27.title: Chinese Officials Vow to Press Political Shake-Up, Saying Corruption Is Focus
2006-09-27.abstract: The investigation is widely seen as an effort by President Hu Jintao to consolidate his own power, rather than a campaign against corruption.

2006-09-28.title: Data Transfer Broke Rules, Report Says
2006-09-28.abstract: A consortium that handles money transfers broke privacy rules when it gave data to the C.I.A., a commission said.

2006-09-28.title: From the Ashes, a Chunk of America Beckons in Somalia
2006-09-28.abstract: In Mogadishu, a Somali woman has created a modest Black Hawk Down museum — a single piece of Black Hawk helicopter wreckage kept in her yard.

2006-09-28.title: France: One Small Operation for Mankind
2006-09-28.abstract: French doctors performed what they described as the first operation on a human in weightless conditions, using an Airbus 300 adapted to simulate conditions in space. In the three-hour flight over southwest France, surgeons and anesthetists in harnesses successfully removed a cyst from the arm of a 46-year-old volunteer strapped to an operating table. Their instruments were held in place by magnets. The operation was part of a program backed by the European Space Agency to develop techniques for performing surgery aboard the International Space Station or other space vehicles.

2006-09-28.title: Wartime Leader of Bosnian Serbs Receives 27-Year Sentence
2006-09-28.abstract: Momcilo Krajisnik was convicted of crimes against humanity for his role in the violent campaign to racially purify large parts of Bosnia.

2006-09-28.title: Georgia Arrests Russians, Charging Them With Spying
2006-09-28.abstract: Top stories from Russian newspapers on Thursday.

2006-09-29.title: France: Smoke ’Em if You’ve Got ’Em. Tout De Suite!
2006-09-29.abstract: France will bar smoking in public places beginning in January but will probably adopt transition rules for restaurants and bars, the health minister, Xavier Bertrand, said in an interview with the newspaper Metro.

2006-09-29.title: South Korean Favored to Win Top Job at U.N.
2006-09-29.abstract: Ban Ki-moon, South Korea’s foreign minister, is closer to becoming the United Nations’ next leader after a Security Council poll.

2006-09-29.title: In Fragile Oysterdom, Pain and Perhaps Some Politics
2006-09-29.abstract: Oysters are France’s sacred fruit, but a pollution alert this summer reminded all of France just how fragile its oyster supply is.

2006-09-29.title: Veteran French Socialist Steps Aside as Candidate for President
2006-09-29.abstract: Lionel Jospin, a former prime minister and a fixture in French politics for nearly three decades, said he would abandon his quest.

2006-09-29.title: Berlin Opera May Be Staged if Police Offer More Security
2006-09-29.abstract: A German opera house said it might reinstate a production it had canceled for fear of provoking a violent reaction from Muslims.

2006-09-30.title: U.N. Says Israel Blocks Inquiry of Post Attack
2006-09-30.abstract: The United Nations said Israel has refused to give its investigators access to the military commanders responsible for directing a precision-guided bomb attack on a post in southern Lebanon on July 25 that killed four unarmed observers despite more than a dozen warnings during the day from United Nations officials that its people were under fire. Israel has said the airstrike was an error.

2006-09-30.title: Iraqi Linked to Sunni Bloc Is Held in Plot, Military Says
2006-09-30.abstract: The Iraqi prime minister’s office took the unusual step of banning all vehicle and pedestrian traffic in Baghdad until Sunday morning.

2006-09-30.title: Al Qaeda Increasingly Reliant on Media
2006-09-30.abstract: Al Qaeda’s increased reliance on various forms of media have made Web-savvy sympathizers like Abu Omar ever more important.

2006-09-30.title: Gaza Strip: Israeli Strike Kills 2 Brothers
2006-09-30.abstract: An Israeli military strike killed two Palestinian brothers, 16 and 13, as they approached a rocket launcher near northern Beit Hanun. The military said the two were there to collect the launcher, used to fire five rockets into southern Israel in recent days. Medical workers at Kamal Adwan Hospital said it was not clear whether the boys rode their bike toward the launcher to retrieve it or out of curiosity.

2006-09-30.title: Georgia: Court Charges 4 Russians With Spying
2006-09-30.abstract: Four Russian intelligence officers accused this week of espionage faced a court in Tbilisi, the capital, and were ordered to be held for two months. A fifth officer had been released earlier. The police and security forces continued to surround the Russian military headquarters in the capital, in an attempt to detain another officer accused of spying. The case has severely strained the already tense relationship between Russia and Georgia. On Thursday, Russia recalled its ambassador from Tbilisi, and yesterday it flew out more than 70 diplomatic staff members and their families, Interfax reported.
